definition: Use an inverted-file or product-quantized vector index to retrieve approximate top-k train neighbors for each evaluation embedding. This is useful when the embedding table is too large for exact storage or brute-force matrix multiplication.

scale_profile.best_for: Large dense-vector similarity where memory and latency matter more than exact pair enumeration.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Millions to billions of vectors with appropriate FAISS indexes and hardware.
scale_profile.avoid_when: Quantization error could change the scientific interpretation and no exact-recall check is possible.

tunable_parameters.name: nprobe
tunable_parameters.purpose: Number of partitions searched at query time.

quality_gates: Benchmark recall@k against exact search on a subset.
Sweep nprobe or equivalent until the spectral curve is stable.
failure_modes: Coarse partitioning can miss neighbors near partition boundaries.
Product quantization can distort fine similarity rankings.
GPU and CPU index variants may return slightly different tradeoffs.
example_usage: Use dense_vector_topk_similarity.py as an exact-contract reference; in production emit the same columns from a FAISS IndexIVF, HNSW, or PQ search.

citations.title: The Faiss library
citations.evidence: Describes FAISS as a toolkit for vector search, clustering, compression, CPU/GPU search, and indexes such as IVF, HNSW, and PQ.

citations.title: ANN-Benchmarks: A Benchmarking Tool for Approximate Nearest Neighbor Algorithms
citations.evidence: Supports recall-versus-throughput evaluation for approximate nearest-neighbor algorithms.
